items.front: When may radar services be provided?
items.back: Provide radar service only when you are personally satisfied the presentation and equipment performance are adequate, and report malfunctions or unexpected outages to the OS/CIC.

items.front: What are the main limits on using secondary radar as the sole display source?
items.back: Use approved surveillance sources. Outside Class A, secondary-only use is limited to the listed cases, and terminal controllers may not use it for ASR final approaches unless an emergency exists and the pilot concurs.

items.front: What must you do if EA activity interferes with radar operations?
items.back: Request suspension through the proper path: directly with the military unit or aircraft en route, or through the ARTCC in terminal operations, using emergency frequency if an immediate stop is required.

items.front: Which aircraft require merging target procedures?
items.back: Apply them to all radar-identified aircraft at 10,000 feet and above, all turbojets, and presidential aircraft, except when the aircraft are established in a holding pattern.

items.front: What is required when aircraft are holding in depicted outer-fix airspace?
items.back: Provide surveillance of the holding area, watch for aircraft that stray outside it, and help them return to assigned airspace if they do.

items.front: When is a deviation advisory required?
items.back: Issue it when an aircraft is observed on a position and track that will obviously cause it to leave protected airspace, and help the aircraft return if needed.

items.front: When is manual fix posting required en route?
items.back: When the EAS FDP flight-progress recording components are not operational, manually record observed or reported time over a fix at least once for each controlled aircraft in your sector.

items.front: What happens to compulsory position reporting after radar contact is established?
items.back: After radar contact is issued, compulsory reporting over fixes stops and resumes only when ATC says radar contact is lost or radar service is terminated.

items.front: When does radar service terminate automatically without a separate advisory?
items.back: It ends automatically in the listed cases, such as certain IFR cancellations, landing or advisory-frequency release on approach, and completion of some VFR or radar approaches.
